snapshot.scope: Independent Product Planning review binds private-network reachability to exact Permission Broker external_network authority. Default Source Fetch still blocks private targets; an authorized action no longer receives a second class-based denial, while scheme, URL credential, per-hop resolution and address pinning, redirect, size, deadline, redaction and untrusted-content controls remain.

reviewers.independence: Product Planning owns only documentation and independently compares the Development-only Source Fetch and capability-adapter bytes with Permission Broker, privacy, transport, trace and test contracts.

dimensions.summary: Permission Broker remains the sole authority owner; Source Fetch receives only an internal authorized/public mode and adds no renderer, storage, Job or secret owner.
Security, runtime policy, parser, architecture, privacy and decision owners agree that private targets block without authority and work after exact authority while all other fetch controls remain.
PIGE-PI-003 and B5.02 wording reflects the new authority rule without promoting any Requirement, Build, Exit, Phase or release status.
Focused Source Fetch and first-party capability tests cover default denial, authorized private and metadata targets, redirects, credentials and unchanged bounded projections.
One security owner holds the full rule; short projections and compact trace updates keep governed Markdown and trace manifests within their exact byte budgets.

verification.command: Independent review of exact external_network private-target authority
verification.evidence: Review confirms public_only remains fail-closed, permissioned_external_network reaches private classes across redirects, and schemes, credentials, pinning, bounds, redaction and content distrust remain independent controls.

verification.command: npm run verify:docs && npm run verify:decisions && npm run verify:traceability
verification.evidence: Links, whitespace, document map, PRD structure, decisions and locked claims pass with no Requirement, Build, Exit or Phase promotion.

verification.command: npm run verify:contracts && npm run verify:independent-documentation-review && npm run verify:documentation-leanness
verification.evidence: CON-001 through CON-004 remain green; focused UI tests, typecheck, build and visual-contract verification bind the exact repair while the union recipe retains the published Reader, Update, release and security roots.

coordination.compatibility: The candidate changes only Source Fetch target-class policy after exact external_network authorization; no renderer, IPC, persistence, secret, Job, model, destructive or filesystem authority changes.
coordination.acknowledgement: Product Planning records the user-authoritative network boundary and keeps Development code/test ownership separate, with no broad status promotion.

nonBlockingRisks: Authorization can expose services on the user's local or infrastructure networks; the explicit Broker decision and operation history remain the user-visible control.
No Requirement, Build, Exit or Phase status is promoted; broader production callers, modal convergence, bypass coverage and signed-platform evidence remain open.
